title: let, const, and var
description: How to declare variables — and why const is the default.

questions.explanation: const binds the name to a value permanently — you can't reassign.
Reassigning a const throws TypeError: Assignment to constant variable.
const forbids REBINDING the variable. It doesn't freeze the object it points to.

questions.prompt: Modern best practice for a value you'll never reassign:
questions.explanation: Default to const. Reach for let only when reassignment is needed. Avoid var.
